title: 10 HubSpot Alternatives For Small Businesses and Startups
author: Caylin White

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: As a startup, founder, entrepreneur orsmall and medium business (SMB)owner, you know the value of good tools. You may know that finding the rightcustomer relationship management (CRM)for your small business means choosing the solution that supports your growthandfits your budget — without any hidden costs. If you’ve used HubSpot or are considering alternatives, this guide is for you. We’ve compiled an ultimate list of CRM tools — from Salesforce to Zoho — ‌that can offer similar features at more affordable prices. Whether you need a simpler tool or a more specialized one, this comprehensive list will help you make an informed decision. Let’s jump right ‌in. What you’ll learn: Why do small businesses need a CRM?Why consider alternatives to HubSpot?Ten HubSpot alternatives for small and growing businessesCRM resources to help you make a decisionSalesforce — your best HubSpot alternative for small business

inline_links.text: Why do small businesses need a CRM?
Ten HubSpot alternatives for small and growing businesses
CRM resources to help you make a decision
Salesforce — your best HubSpot alternative for small business

sections.heading: Why do small businesses need a CRM?
sections.text: Small and growing businesses need a CRM to get a 360-degree view of their operations. A CRM serves as the central platform that connectsmarketing,sales,commerce,service,andproductivityefforts. The right CRM unifies customer data and automation, so that business runs smoothly. And, if you’re here, you know that there are many CRMs to choose from.

sections.heading: Why consider alternatives to HubSpot?
sections.text: HubSpot’s fluctuating pricing changes can be a significant inconvenience. SMBs could be paying up to $800 per month for basic features like reporting and automation. In fact, in aSalesforce vs HubSpotcomparison, customers who switch from HubSpot see over 31% ‌increased revenue and 29% ‌return on investment (ROI). HubSpot’s comprehensive suite requires investment across multiple hubs (marketing, sales, and service) and their core features are locked in higher tiers. Small teams might find HubSpot too complicated and confusing, as it requires onboarding assistance and specialized expertise to operate. And, HubSpot’s basic plans restrict key features for growing businesses like API access andautomation.

inline_links.text: Small teams might find HubSpot too complicated and confusing

sections.heading: Top 10 HubSpot alternatives for growing businesses
sections.text: We’ve selected the top HubSpot CRM alternatives with an average star rating of 4.2 or above on thesoftware comparison website G2. We’ve also rated each based onscalability in a 1-10 ranking, a key factor for your business’s future growth.

Its drag-and-drop, and out-of-the-box features help you grow without technical expertise. Use prebuilt templates andemail marketing automationsto send, monitor, and optimize your strategy. Customize your pipelines and workflows to fit your needs, while AI provides insights to improve customer interactions. sections.heading: Agentforce for Small Business is Now Built Into Salesforce Suites
sections.text: Small businesses can put AI to work directly inside Salesforce Suites, now powered by Agentforce, turning customer data and context into action across every team. Source:Streak CRM Streak CRMis a CRM platform specifically integrated with Gmail. It offers core elements like contact management andlink trackingto analyze customer behavior, and brings your team together with shared pipelines. Who it’s for: Freelancers and small businesses What it can do: Monitor sales activities: Track all sales activities — emails, calls, and meetings — to identify bottlenecks and improvement opportunities.Collaboration tools:Share pipelines, contacts, and notes to make sure your team can seamlessly collaborate on deals or projects.Data management: Streak can capture contact information from emails and public sources — wherever yourtarget audienceengages with you. How it benefits SMBs:Streak CRM helps you run your business without leaving your Gmail account. It helps you prioritize high-value leads through personalized pipelines that highlight hot prospects by deal size and due date. Streak automatically collects contact details from emails and improves conversions with personalized sequences and engagement tracking. Bring your team together with shared pipelines, call logs, and voice notes that integrate with Slack andCalendly. Ultimately, you become more efficient by syncing tasks and to-do lists seamlessly withGoogle Calendar. How it scales business (1-10):5 What it costs:$49 per user per month Source:Zendesk Zendeskis a comprehensive, AI-enabled customer service and sales platform. It’s known for its human resource capabilities, but it’s like having one big inbox for all your customer questions — whether by phone, email, chat, or social media. Who it’s for: Startups and small businesses What it can do: Omni-channel service: Zendesk centralizes customer interactions from various channels, including email, live chat, social media, and messaging apps for anomni-channel servicestrategy.Quality assurance: Zendesk helps you monitor and improve the quality of customer interactions for peak efficiency.Ticketing system:Organizes customer inquiries into “tickets,” ensuring no issue is overlooked. How it benefits SMBs:Zendesk offers multiple features to improve youromni-channel support. Its centralized dashboard makes sure all team members have the latest information to makedata-driven decisions. Also, in-app and website messaging makes it convenient for customers to reach you from wherever they are. Improve your team’s productivity as Zendesk integrates with apps likeSalesforce,Google Play, and Slack. Don’t forget to take advantage of Zendesk’sStartup Community, whichoffersSMBs with fundingfree access to its Slack community and product updates. How it scales business (1-10):6 What it costs:$19 per user per month Source:Zoho Zohois a CRM platform with different suites for sales, commerce, marketing, and customer service. It comes with no-code tools and intuitive workflows to help businesses quickly set up operations. Zoho also offers a comprehensive view of your customer interactions to help you deliver consistent services. Who it’s for: Growing businesses and startups What it can do: Unified marketing dashboard:Marketing campaigns are more organized with all interactions and engagement data in one place. Zoho captures leads from web forms and converts visitors into contacts.Activity management: Collaborate on tasks, meetings, and calls through Zoho’s shared calendar. Customize this dashboard with fields and reports for your business needs.Reporting and analytics:Prebuilt and customizable dashboards are available to track key sales metrics and analyze your overall sales performance. How it benefits SMBs:Zoho helps SMBs move beyond spreadsheets with improved sales tracking through visual dashboards that unify sales and marketing performance. Achieve highermarketing ROIby tracking campaign stages — from content creation to performance. Deliver consistent and uniform customer service as a single dashboard provides a complete view of customer behavior and preferences. How it scales business (1-10):9 What it costs: $7 per user per month and aforever-free trial.

sections.heading: CRM resources to help you make a decision
sections.text: Making the right CRM choice is important. So, we’ve compiled valuable resources fortified by Salesforce research, to guide your decision: The State of CRM Reporthighlights how businesses can use a CRM as a single source of truth. ACRM for startupsboosts productivity and communication, while tapping into opportunities forsustainable growth. With this free report, learn about the challenges global businesses face and how CRMs can address them. What should I look for in a CRM? Does this CRM solve all my business needs? Why are thriving businesses picking Salesforce, and what are itstop benefits? Get answers to these questions and more with ourCRM Buyer’s Guidevideo series. This quick playlist will help you make an informed choice. And even better? You’ll hear straight from one of our customers who share what made them pick Salesforce. AI is crucial for the growth of any modern business, small or large.Salesforce’s AI Researchhelps our customers constantly innovate and prepare for the future. We followethical AIguidelines and develop AI tools with immense responsibility. Get all the latest information on our research and developments likeagentic AIor Agentforce 360 — and how we bring these to our CRM. Salesforce’sTrailblazerCommunity is a global network of Salesforce users, entrepreneurs, and enthusiasts. It’s a vibrant platform where individuals can ask questions or seekmentorshipand collaboration — all while staying updated with the latest technologies and trends. You can also attend virtual community events by joining different groups. The goal is to network, learn, and help each other succeed no matter where you are. SMB owners can connect with like-minded entrepreneurs onSalesforce Small and Medium Business Hubor on theSalesforce Partners page. That’s a wrap! Here’s the quick-hit list of CRM alternatives:

inline_links.text: Salesforce Small and Medium Business Hub

sections.heading: The best HubSpot alternative for small business? We vote for Salesforce
sections.text: A CRM is your business partner, helping you cross significant milestones on your journey. Whether you’re looking to start out or for more automation or features, there are many alternatives to HubSpot. Salesforce is built to meet all your evolving needs. No matter where you’re on your journey as a small business owner, you can get started withStarter Suite— the all-in-one AI CRM your SMB needs. Looking for more customization?Explore Pro Suite. Already a Salesforce customer? ActivateFoundationsand try out Agentforce 360 today. AI supported the writers and editors who created this article.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
sections.text: The three basic CRM functionalities arecontact management,sales management, andcustomer service management. When choosing a CRM, ensure the tool offers these three functionalities. Some popular alternatives to HubSpot CRM include Salesforce, Zoho CRM, and Freshsales. Each offers uniquefeaturesand pricing models to suit different business needs. To choose the right HubSpot CRM alternative, consider your specific business needs, budget, and the features that are most important to you, such assales automation, marketing tools, and customer service capabilities. It’s also helpful to read reviews and try out free trials to see which CRM fits best. While some CRMs offer free versions with limited features, ‌most require a paid subscription for comprehensive solutions. Check out the free trials offered by Salesforce, Pipedrive, and Keap before deciding. Salesforce is considered the best CRM for small businesses due to its comprehensive features like trust, security, and data management. It’s trusted by businesses of all sizes — from startups to Fortune 500 companies — due to its consistent performance and commitment to ethical business practices. Zoho CRM is also a great CRM that offers affordability and high customization.

raw_html:
As a startup, founder, entrepreneur or small and medium business (SMB) owner, you know the value of good tools. You may know that finding the right customer relationship management (CRM) for your small business means choosing the solution that supports your growth and fits your budget — without any hidden costs.  

If you’ve used HubSpot or are considering alternatives, this guide is for you. We’ve compiled an ultimate list of CRM tools — from Salesforce to Zoho — ‌that can offer similar features at more affordable prices. Whether you need a simpler tool or a more specialized one, this comprehensive list will help you make an informed decision. Let’s jump right ‌in. 

What you’ll learn:

  • Why do small businesses need a CRM?
  • Why consider alternatives to HubSpot?
  • Ten HubSpot alternatives for small and growing businesses
  • CRM resources to help you make a decision
  • Salesforce — your best HubSpot alternative for small business

Why do small businesses need a CRM?

Small and growing businesses need a CRM to get a 360-degree view of their operations. A CRM serves as the central platform that connects marketing, sales, commerce, service, and productivity efforts. The right CRM unifies customer data and automation, so that business runs smoothly. And, if you’re here, you know that there are many CRMs to choose from.

Why consider alternatives to HubSpot?

HubSpot’s fluctuating pricing changes can be a significant inconvenience. SMBs could be paying up to $800 per month for basic features like reporting and automation. In fact, in a Salesforce vs HubSpot comparison, customers who switch from HubSpot see over 31% ‌increased revenue and 29% ‌return on investment (ROI). HubSpot’s comprehensive suite requires investment across multiple hubs (marketing, sales, and service) and their core features are locked in higher tiers.

Small teams might find HubSpot too complicated and confusing, as it requires onboarding assistance and specialized expertise to operate. And, HubSpot’s basic plans restrict key features for growing businesses like API access and automation.

Start with SMB Basics

Get the Free SMB Trends Report

How to Automate Your Small Business (AI Tips and Tools)

Win More Deals with Salesforce Starter Suite

Starter Suite vs. Pro Suite: CRM Comparison For SMBs

Top 10 HubSpot alternatives for growing businesses

We’ve selected the top HubSpot CRM alternatives with an average star rating of 4.2 or above on the software comparison website G2. We’ve also rated each based on scalability in a 1-10 ranking, a key factor for your business’s future growth.

1. Salesforce Starter Suite

Salesforce Starter Suite is the ideal cloud-based CRM tool for small businesses and startups. It offers comprehensive business management tools without the complexity or high costs of enterprise solutions. This affordable, easy to use platform helps you manage leads, sales, and build your online store. 

Starter Suite packs features across marketing, sales, service, commerce, and productivity, into a single, integrated platform. Its drag-and-drop, and out-of-the-box features help you grow without technical expertise. It’s a great starting point for any type of SMB that wants to build a sustainable business without breaking the bank.

Who it’s for: Small and growing businesses, entrepreneurs, startups, and those looking to get started with CRM

What it can do:

  • Sales management: Capture and organize sales leads to nurture and convert them through the sales pipeline — from initial interest to final purchase decision.
  • Personalized marketing: Multiple tools and templates for email marketing and landing pages. Also, get insights on improving your marketing performance.
  • 360-degree customer view: Unify your customer interactions, including case management, to offer a more personalized customer experience. 
  • Commerce storefront: Launch your online store with our storefront builder that offers prebuilt templates, and integrated payment and checkout solutions.
  • Mobile access: Access your CRM data on the go with the Salesforce Mobile App, so you can resolve ‌issues from anywhere, enhancing overall satisfaction.
  • Easy onboarding: Starter Suite’s built-in onboarding guides, video tutorials, and help center will help you whenever needed.

Source: Sandy Alexander drives faster CRM adoption with Salesforce Starter Suite

How it benefits SMBs: Starter Suite helps you with sales leads, audience segmentation and lead scoring to prioritize and close deals faster. Your marketing efforts improve helping you create and optimize campaigns. Customer satisfaction rises with data-driven insights, and more personalized support. Automated workflows and integrated tools like Slack simplify operations, saving you time and boosting productivity. Your data is always safe, while maintaining customer trust and cybersecurity.

How it scales business (1-10): 10

What it costs: $25 per user per month and offers a free 30-day trial. 

2. Salesforce Pro Suite

Salesforce Pro Suite offers all the features of Starter Suite (and more) and is a scalable CRM to support more complex business needs. In addition to marketing, sales, service, and commerce support, it offers advanced customizations, integrations, and automation. As your business matures, Pro Suite provides analytics tools and workflows to scale without a major upfront investment.

Who it’s for: Growing midsize businesses, startups competing with large enterprises, and small businesses scaling fast

What it can do: 

  • Sales and marketing automation: Manage sales opportunities effectively with built-in stages, and customize pricing quotes to optimize your sales cycle and predict revenue. Manage multiple channels with prebuilt templates, and automated marketing workflows.
  • Omnichannel customer service: Integrated support with omnichannel service options, chatbots, and custom email templates.
  • Commerce automation: Storefront website design, managed checkout and insight dashboards.
  • Built-in analytics: Create custom reports and dashboards to gain deeper insights into your key performance indicators (KPIs).
  • Integrations: Access Salesforce AppExchange, a marketplace for all apps and integrations to extend your CRM capabilities. 
  • Dedicated CRM support: Pro Suite offers an onboarding specialist to guide you through the setup process and to customize the suite to your needs.

Source: Salesforce Pro Suite

How it benefits SMBs: Businesses can scale effortlessly with sales productivity tools, built-in dashboards and sales forecasting. Get a clear view of your financial health, and convert your prospects faster and a unified view of your marketing performance across all channels, ‌including social media promotions and omni-channel reach. With self-service and chatbots to deliver faster, more contextual customer service and integrated survey tools, you can gather customer feedback regularly and enhance your offerings. Take your ecommerce to the next level with merchandising tools and customized pricing structures. And your data is safe with multi-factor authentication (MFA) and data encryption.

How it scales business (1-10): 10 

What it costs: $100 per user per month and a free 30-day trial. 

Ready to go Pro?

Level up your CRM with the top-rated Pro Suite — the customer platform that scales with you.

Try it for free

3. ActiveCampaign

Source: ActiveCampaign

ActiveCampaign is a cloud-based CRM tool that helps businesses manage their customer relationships across various digital channels. It offers a variety of marketing automation features that help lean teams manage their cross-channel marketing strategy.

Who it’s for: Solo founders and startups looking for a marketing-focused tool

What it can do: 

  • Omni-channel marketing: Handle omni-channel marketing and interact with customers on platforms of their choice.
  • Email and content marketing: Use drag-and-drop features to set up email campaigns, and create content. A/B test different headlines, and call-to-action (CTA) buttons to know what’s working.
  • Quick automations: Simplify your marketing and sales efforts through prebuilt automation recipes and workflows.  

What are the benefits: ActiveCampaign can help you with multiple marketing activities. Segment customers at every stage of the sales funnel to help your sales team identify and pursue hot leads. Create and schedule engaging marketing content like advertisements, social media, and SMS campaigns. Save time and get faster operations by automating tasks like contact tagging and drip marketing. Your operations run smoothly as ActiveCampaign integrates with key tools like Salesforce, Google Analytics, and Slack. Also, get personalized support and hands-on learning through interactive webinars and learning sessions

How it scales business (1-10): 6 

What it costs: $15 per user per month, and it offers a free 14-day trial. 

Seeing is believing. Try Salesforce for free with a 30 day trial.

See how small businesses are using Salesforce CRM to scale fast. No credit card required, no software to install. It all starts with Starter Suite.

4. EngageBay

Source: EngageBay

EngageBay is a CRM that unifies marketing, sales, and customer support capabilities into a single platform. It provides features like live chat and a social media suite to help with content marketing.

Who it’s for: Small businesses and digital creators

What it can do: 

  • Email and lead management: Use built-in email templates to create and schedule your email marketing campaigns. Also, score lead quality and use predictive analytics to identify conversions.
  • Contact segmentation: Segment your contacts by demographics, behavior, and engagement to tailor your communications.
  • Page builder: Create mobile-ready landing pages and customize elements like CTA buttons, fonts, and headings to match your brand guidelines.

How it benefits SMBs: EngageBay helps SMBs acquire new customers effortlessly with email tracking. This helps your sales team track and follow up on leads throughout the funnel. It offers prebuilt email templates and social media tracking to boost open rates and click-through rates (CTRs). EngageBay improves customer experience with a unified customer view, allowing service teams to offer faster support. Collaborate seamlessly with project management dashboards and file sharing within an easy to use interface.

How it scales business (1-10): 6

What it costs: $12 per user per month 

5. Freshsales Suite

Source: Freshsales Suite

Freshsales Suite is an AI-powered CRM with a focus on sales processes. It helps you organize customer data and manage your pipeline, bringing sales and marketing teams together. This collaboration enables faster deal closures and uniform customer experiences.

Who it’s for: Small businesses, and startups looking for a sales-focused CRM

What it can do: 

  • Multichannel marketing: Deliver consistent customer experiences across email, social media, SMS, and chat. 
  • AI insights: AI can score and prioritize leads based on their likelihood to convert, helping you focus on high-potential opportunities. 
  • Multiple integrations: Access Freshworks Marketplace for project management, accounting, and documentation apps.

How it benefits SMBs: Freshsales Suite brings your customer interactions in one place, and simplifies your sales processes. It helps increase your average order value (AOV) and revenue through insights into cross-selling and upselling opportunities. Improve lead nurturing and customer acquisition with AI-driven insights.

How it scales business (1-10): 6 

What it costs: $9 per user per month and  a free 21-day trial.

6. Keap

Source: Keap

Keap is a marketing and sales CRM platform, and calls itself the “#1 HubSpot alternative.” Like Starter Suite, Keap provides a 360-degree view of customer interactions and business performance. It offers various marketing automation features and helps with client management and sales workflows. 

Who it’s for: Entrepreneurs and small businesses

What it can do: 

  • Personalized marketing and sales: Prebuilt workflows and automation for follow-ups and lead nurturing speed up your sales cycle.
  • Automated customer management: It centralizes all customer and lead information for a complete overview. Automatically captures leads from websites and social media while handling follow-up communications.
  • Reporting and analytics: Set up reporting dashboards to monitor sales and marketing performance to improve your SMB sales strategy.

How it benefits SMBs: Keap unifies sales and payment processing on a single platform as it comes with built-in marketing automation and post-purchase communications. Save time by automating repetitive tasks like contact categorization and follow-ups. This lets you focus on strategic growth. Deliver continuous customer engagement through the mobile app and by setting up automatic replies for faster issue resolution. Also, Keap integrates with apps for project management, data storage, and ecommerce to create a cohesive relationship between all your tools. 

How it scales business (1-10): 5 

What it costs: Minimum of $249 for two users per month and a free 14-day trial.

7. Pipedrive

Source: Pipedrive

Pipedrive is a simple, sales-focused AI CRM with features for managing sales pipelines and deal closures. It allows you to manage your communications, reports, and analytics all in one place. Pipedrive comes with chatbot support to answer your questions and provide any assistance.

Who it’s for: Small businesses

What it can do: 

  • Optimized email marketing: Track key marketing metrics like bounce rate, open rates, and customer engagement. 
  • Efficient lead management: Pipeline’s visual interface simplifies lead management. It allows you to capture, qualify, and prioritize leads while easily moving deals through the deal stages.
  • AI analytics: Reporting and analytics dashboards track your sales and business performance. AI recommends tools and actions to enhance your business operations.

How it benefits SMBs: Close deals faster with visual dashboards that track sales cycles and improve revenue forecasting. Use prebuilt templates and email marketing automations to send, monitor, and optimize your strategy. Customize your pipelines and workflows to fit your needs, while AI provides insights to improve customer interactions. Simplify your operations with AI assistants that write emails, track interactions, and predict revenue. Also, stay productive with timely team reminders via email, in-app, or push notifications directly within your CRM.

How it scales business (1-10): 7

What it costs: $14 per user per month and a free 14-day trial

8. Streak CRM

Source: Streak CRM

Streak CRM is a CRM platform specifically integrated with Gmail. It offers core elements like contact management and link tracking to analyze customer behavior, and brings your team together with shared pipelines.

Who it’s for: Freelancers and small businesses

What it can do:

  • Monitor sales activities: Track all sales activities — emails, calls, and meetings — to identify bottlenecks and improvement opportunities.
  • Collaboration tools: Share pipelines, contacts, and notes to make sure your team can seamlessly collaborate on deals or projects. 
  • Data management: Streak can capture contact information from emails and public sources — wherever your target audience engages with you.

How it benefits SMBs: Streak CRM helps you run your business without leaving your Gmail account. It helps you prioritize high-value leads through personalized pipelines that highlight hot prospects by deal size and due date. Streak automatically collects contact details from emails and improves conversions with personalized sequences and engagement tracking. Bring your team together with shared pipelines, call logs, and voice notes that integrate with Slack and Calendly. Ultimately, you become more efficient by syncing tasks and to-do lists seamlessly with Google Calendar.

How it scales business (1-10): 5 

What it costs: $49 per user per month

9. Zendesk

Source: Zendesk

Zendesk is a comprehensive, AI-enabled customer service and sales platform. It’s known for its human resource capabilities, but it’s like having one big inbox for all your customer questions — whether by phone, email, chat, or social media.

Who it’s for: Startups and small businesses 

What it can do: 

  • Omni-channel service: Zendesk centralizes customer interactions from various channels, including email, live chat, social media, and messaging apps for an omni-channel service strategy. 
  • Quality assurance: Zendesk helps you monitor and improve the quality of customer interactions for peak efficiency.
  • Ticketing system: Organizes customer inquiries into “tickets,” ensuring no issue is overlooked.

How it benefits SMBs: Zendesk offers multiple features to improve your omni-channel support. Its centralized dashboard makes sure all team members have the latest information to make data-driven decisions. Also, in-app and website messaging makes it convenient for customers to reach you from wherever they are. Improve your team’s productivity as Zendesk integrates with apps like Salesforce, Google Play, and Slack. Don’t forget to take advantage of Zendesk’s Startup Community, which offers SMBs with funding free access to its Slack community and product updates.

How it scales business (1-10): 6 

What it costs: $19 per user per month

10. Zoho

Source: Zoho

Zoho is a CRM platform with different suites for sales, commerce, marketing, and customer service. It comes with no-code tools and intuitive workflows to help businesses quickly set up operations. Zoho also offers a comprehensive view of your customer interactions to help you deliver consistent services. 

Who it’s for: Growing businesses and startups

What it can do: 

  • Unified marketing dashboard: Marketing campaigns are more organized with all interactions and engagement data in one place. Zoho captures leads from web forms and converts visitors into contacts.
  • Activity management: Collaborate on tasks, meetings, and calls through Zoho’s shared calendar. Customize this dashboard with fields and reports for your business needs.
  • Reporting and analytics: Prebuilt and customizable dashboards are available to track key sales metrics and analyze your overall sales performance.

How it benefits SMBs: Zoho helps SMBs move beyond spreadsheets with improved sales tracking through visual dashboards that unify sales and marketing performance. Achieve higher marketing ROI by tracking campaign stages — from content creation to performance. Deliver consistent and uniform customer service as a single dashboard provides a complete view of customer behavior and preferences. 

How it scales business (1-10): 9 

What it costs: $7 per user per month and a forever-free trial.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

The three basic CRM functionalities are contact management, sales management, and customer service management. When choosing a CRM, ensure the tool offers these three functionalities.

Some popular alternatives to HubSpot CRM include Salesforce, Zoho CRM, and Freshsales. Each offers unique features and pricing models to suit different business needs.

To choose the right HubSpot CRM alternative, consider your specific business needs, budget, and the features that are most important to you, such as sales automation, marketing tools, and customer service capabilities. It’s also helpful to read reviews and try out free trials to see which CRM fits best.

While some CRMs offer free versions with limited features, ‌most require a paid subscription for comprehensive solutions. Check out the free trials offered by Salesforce, Pipedrive, and Keap before deciding.

Salesforce is considered the best CRM for small businesses due to its comprehensive features like trust, security, and data management. It’s trusted by businesses of all sizes — from startups to Fortune 500 companies — due to its consistent performance and commitment to ethical business practices. Zoho CRM is also a great CRM that offers affordability and high customization.
